concept:
Tested 3 ? Most common cause of hypothyroidism is: Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
Exophthalmos
Pretibial myxedema
Decreased TSH are indications of: Graves' disease
First line treatment for growth hormone secreting pituitary adenoma is: Transsphenoidal surgical resection.
Source of lesion in Cushing's syndrome with high ACTH levels is: Either
Ectopic
Pituitary .
Most common two causes of Cushing's syndrome are:
First most common: Latrogenic steroid administration
Second most common: Cushing's desease
Patient presents with
Signs of hypocalcemia
High phosphorus
Low PTH indicating: Hypoparathyroidism
"Stones, bones, groans and psychiatric overtones" are signs of: Hypercalcemia
Patient
complains of
Headache
Weakness
Polyuria
exam reveals
Hypertension (HTN)
Tetany
lab reveals
Hypernatremia
Hypokalemia
Metabolic alkalosis
indicating: Primary hyperaldosteronism due to Conn's syndrome or bilateral adrenal hyperplasia
Patient presents with:
Tachycardia
Wild swings in blood pressure (BP)
Headache
Diaphoresis
Altered mental status
A sense of panic indicating: Pheochromocytoma
Should
alpha
beta antagonists be used first in treating pheochromocytoma? Alpha antagonists (phentolamine and phenoxybenzamine) Beta antagonists
Patient with a history of lithium use presents with copius amounts of dilute urine indicating: Nephregenic diabetes insipidus (DI)
Diagnostic test that helps differentiate central from nephregenic diabetes insipidus (DI) is: Administration of DDAVP and measurement of urine osmolality
Patient
is postoperative
has significant pain
has hyponatremia
has normal volume status indicating: SIADH due to stress
The only indication for administering hypertonic saline to a patient with SIADH is: Active seizures
Antidiabetic agent associated with lactic acidosis is: Metformin
Morning hyperglycemia that is a rebound response to nighttime hypoglycemia, not a need for more insulin. Treatment? Somogyi effect. Reduce PM insulin dose
Patient
presents with
weakness
nausea
vomiting
weight loss
a new skin pigmentation
labs show
hyponatremia
hyperkalemia
. Treatment? Addison's disease. Treat with
replacement glucocorticoids
mineralocorticoids .
Goal hemoglobin A(1c) for a patient with diabetes mellitus (DM) is? Less than 7.0
Treatment for Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) is:
Fluids
Insulin
Aggressive replacement of electrolytes (e.g. K+)
In obese patients with this disorder, weight loss may completely eliminate the need for medication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (DM)
Why are beta blockers contraindicated in diabetics? They can mask symptoms of hypoglycemia
Most common cause of pathologic fractures in elderly thin women is? Osteoporosis
